scope: Build-ready identity, chapter-gate, topology, transformation, and interpolation evidence for Little Garden, Drum Island, Jaya, Long Ring Long Land, and Thriller Bark. No manga panels, anime frames, copied maps, GLBs, or runtime registrations are included.

source_policy.official_arc_summaries: ONE PIECE.com is used for high-level arc/location identity.
source_policy.chapter_receipts: Exact Fandom chapter summary URLs are used to locate the first safe visual/explanation gates; these are evidence pointers, not licensed visual inputs.
source_policy.geometry_policy: Textual topology may support relative systems. Panel/map outlines, dimensions, bearings, and distances remain unmeasured unless explicitly established.
source_policy.copyright_boundary: Do not copy manga panels, anime stills, wiki maps, logos, or textures into generated assets.

local_records.audit: Identity is accepted. The visual model gate is corrected to chapter 115 because chapter 114 only identifies the next destination.
Identity and visual gate agree at chapter 132.
Identity and visual gate agree at chapter 222. Upper Yard is an external related system, not a second Jaya mesh.

record.name: Long Ring Long Land
record.japanese: ロングリングロングランド

local_records.audit: Identity and visual gate agree at chapter 304.

The local hand-authored record correctly calls this a ship but flags its placement/debut for human verification. The geographic system first becomes visually safe at chapter 443. Its atlas point is never a fixed coordinate.

claims.claim: Little Garden is a Paradise tropical island with a prehistoric ecosystem and a century-long giant duel as its defining story geography.
Chapter 115 is the safe first visual gate for the island, tropical forest, prehistoric scale, and arrival geography; chapter 114 only points the crew toward it.
Chapter 117 establishes Dorry and Brogy's recurring duel context, separate camp use, a shared duel space, and old sailor remains; exact camp bearings are not established.
Chapter 118 establishes the interior volcano eruption as the signal for the duel; plume geometry and exact island-center placement are not measurements.
Drum Island is a permanent winter island first safely visible at chapter 132.
The Drum Rockies dominate the island and the tallest central Drum Rock supports Drum Castle, establishing a verified vertical hierarchy without measured heights.
Named villages occupy lower island terrain and a ropeway serves the summit castle; chapter 136 and 142 are conservative build gates, while exact settlement positions and cable span remain unresolved.
An avalanche can be represented as a chapter-136 event proxy, while chapter 153 verifies the one-shot pink-snow/sakura display; neither is permanent terrain.
Present-day Jaya is safely visible from chapter 222 and is the remaining lower-sea part of a formerly larger island.
Mock Town and its port are safe at chapter 223; the official arc summary places Mock Town on Jaya's west side, but exact bearings and footprints remain unmeasured.
Chapter 253 aligns current Jaya and Upper Yard maps into the former skull-like landmass, verifying their relationship but not an exact dimensional 3D transform.
claims.render_policy: Reference the existing skypiea-sky-system Upper Yard component. Do not duplicate its geometry inside the Jaya package.

claims.claim: The separated portion was launched into the sky roughly four hundred years earlier; chapter 292 supports the historical event, which must never animate as a present-day transformation.
Long Ring Long Land is safely visible from chapter 304 and has a distinctive elongated landscape and life-form identity.
Open plains, hills, flora, and fauna share an elongated visual identity; representative proportions are allowed but exact species placement is not geographic evidence.
Chapter 305 explains that ten apparent islands form a ring but are sections of one island connected beneath normal water level.
Low tide exposes the connecting path once each year; chapter 305 unlocks the explanation, not a claim that the tide is active during that chapter.
Thriller Bark is a moving pirate ship built from a former West Blue island and must not be modeled or registered as fixed terrain.
Chapter 443 is the first safe visual gate for the fog-hidden giant mouth gate, enclosing outer structure, and ship/island system trapping the Sunny; chapter 442 alone is not sufficient.

Chapter 444 establishes the shore/dead-forest entry, chapter 445 establishes the graveyard and Hogback mansion, and the island/defense layers sit inside the outer ship wall without exact surveyed bearings.
Chapter 449 makes the enormous pirate-ship identity, middle village, main mast/giant sail, mast mansion, and wall-to-mast chain system safe for the full exterior scene.

allowed: unmeasured path curvature and settlement massing
diagrammatic relationship overlays clearly labeled non-scale
neutral environmental effects that do not claim exact panel choreography
forbidden: canon bearings inferred from derived atlas anchors
panel-exact shoreline tracing without licensed source geometry
present-day Jaya separation or duplicate Upper Yard geometry
chapter-driven Long Ring annual tide without current-state evidence
manga panel, anime still, wiki map, logo, or texture copying

unresolved_geometry.handling: Interpolate coastline/volcano/camps for readable topology; withhold story combat and exact routes.
Interpolate peak/village/river/ropeway layout; withhold post-timeskip castle state until verified.
Interpolate present local layout; use a non-scale external Upper Yard reference only; withhold exact fit transform.
Interpolate section outlines/ring/base; show annual low tide only as an explanatory overlay.
Interpolate nested exterior layout under a moving root; withhold Wonder Garden bridge and all late damage until exact gates are verified.
